title: Fix theme colours (remove all black), fix SparkParticles distribution, restore warm/bright palette

requirements.summary: Completely remove all black and near-black colours from the entire app UI
acceptanceCriteria: No element in the app renders with a black or near-black (#000–#222 range) background or text colour.
Dark-mode CSS rules that produce black surfaces are removed or overridden.
The app remains fully readable with sufficient contrast on all surfaces.

file_operations.description: Remove or override all dark-mode CSS custom properties that produce black or near-black backgrounds and text. Replace any OKLCH values with lightness close to 0% with appropriate light alternatives from the warm palette. Ensure base body styles and border styles do not use black tones.

Audit and remove any OKLCH-based design tokens for light and dark modes that produce black (#000000) or near-black (#111, #1a1a1a, #0a0a0a) colours. Replace all dark background tokens with warm, light alternatives to ensure no element renders with a black surface.

Remove or override any Tailwind custom color tokens (OKLCH-based semantic tokens) that produce black or near-black colours in dark mode. Ensure all semantic tokens use light, warm colour values with sufficient lightness (≥40%) to prevent black surfaces from appearing anywhere in the app.

requirements.summary: Redesign the global colour palette to a warm, bright, friendly theme
acceptanceCriteria: The app uses a warm brown/skin-tone or blue/white colour scheme with no black or dark surfaces.
All Tailwind config colour tokens and CSS custom properties reflect the new palette.
Cards, buttons, badges, modals, header, and footer all use the new palette consistently.
Text on all surfaces has sufficient contrast ratio (≥ 4.5:1) for readability.

file_operations.description: Replace the current OKLCH-based semantic color tokens with a new warm palette. Choose either warm brown/skin-tone (sandy #F5CBA7, warm beige #FAE5D3, terracotta #E8956D accents) or blue/white (sky blue #60A5FA family, white #FFFFFF, soft blue-grey #EFF6FF). Update all primary, secondary, accent, background, foreground, card, border, and muted tokens to reflect the chosen palette. Remove any dark/lavender-heavy or near-black tokens.

Update CSS custom properties to reflect the new warm palette chosen in tailwind.config.js. Replace any dark or lavender-heavy OKLCH tokens with the new warm brown/skin-tone or blue/white values. Ensure the dot-grid background dots use the new palette. Update any animation or spark-float effect colours to match the new theme.

Update the OKLCH-based design tokens for colors, radius, and sidebar to match the new warm palette. Ensure all light and dark mode tokens use the warm brown/skin-tone or blue/white colour scheme with no black or dark surfaces.

Ensure the header, navigation pills, active-route indicator, and footer use the new warm palette tokens. Update any inline styles or className references to use the updated Tailwind tokens. Verify the mute/unmute toggle and year badge also use the new colour scheme.

Update the PostCard component to use the new warm palette for card backgrounds, borders, badges, and text. Ensure the category badge, year label, upvotes, and bookmark button all use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.

Update the YearSelectionModal to use the new warm palette for the modal background, buttons, and text. Ensure all interactive elements (year selection buttons, college name input, submit button) use the updated Tailwind tokens.

Update the Home page to use the new warm palette for the hero banner, category filter tabs, and post card grid. Ensure all buttons, badges, and loading skeletons use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.

Update the ShareExperience page to use the new warm palette for form elements, buttons, and validation messages. Ensure the category selection, textarea, and submit button all use the updated Tailwind tokens.

Update the PostDetail page to use the new warm palette for the post card, category badge, bookmark button, share buttons, and back navigation. Ensure all elements use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.

Update the Dashboard page to use the new warm palette for statistics cards, recent posts, and the College Connect form and list. Ensure all cards, buttons, badges, and loading states use the updated Tailwind tokens.
Update the About page to use the new warm palette for the banner, feature cards, and text. Ensure all elements use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.
Update the Wishlist page to use the new warm palette for the post card grid, loading skeletons, and empty state. Ensure all elements use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.
Update the CollegeConnectForm component to use the new warm palette for form fields, buttons, validation messages, and success feedback. Ensure all elements use the updated Tailwind tokens.
Update the CollegeConnectList component to use the new warm palette for tip cards, year-colored badges, and loading skeletons. Ensure all elements use the updated Tailwind tokens.
Update the ShareButtons component to use the new warm palette for action buttons. Ensure the WhatsApp, copy link, and native share buttons all use the updated Tailwind tokens for consistent styling.

requirements.summary: Fix the SparkParticles component so that the 12–20 floating year-label pills are distributed evenly across the entire viewport
acceptanceCriteria: Spark pills appear across the full viewport width and height, not clustered in one corner or region.
All four year-label phrases are evenly represented across the 16 instances.
Spark pill colours match the new warm palette (no dark or purple tones).
Particles remain pointer-events: none and do not block interactive elements.

file_operations.description: Replace the current seeded-random position calculation with a stratified random or grid-based layout that distributes all 16 particles evenly across the full viewport (0%–95% width, 0%–90% height). Ensure each particle starts at a distinct, well-separated position to prevent clustering. Update the particle colours to use the new warm palette tokens (e.g., warm brown/skin-tone or blue/white) instead of the current pastel purple/dark tones. Maintain pointer-events: none to keep particles non-interactive.
